metadata.title: macOS Sequoia on Unsupported Mac: OCLP Planning Guide
metadata.description: Plan a macOS Sequoia unsupported Mac upgrade with OCLP caveats, model checks, and official source links.

checker.title: Check a Mac before trying macOS Sequoia with OCLP
checker.description: Sequoia planning should start with exact model support, graphics and wireless caveats, and the latest OCLP release notes.

workflow.title: Before planning Sequoia
workflow.description: Newer macOS versions can expose older hardware limits more sharply.

items.title: Verify the model
items.description: Search the exact model identifier in the official supported models page.

items.title: Check graphics support
items.description: Non-Metal or older GPU setups can require special caution.

items.title: Check wireless risk
items.description: Wi-Fi and Bluetooth behavior can differ by card and patch status.

items.title: Use official release notes
items.description: Do not rely on old forum posts for the current Sequoia/OCLP combination.

faq.title: macOS Sequoia OCLP FAQ
faq.description: Common limits for unsupported Mac planning.

items.question: Should every unsupported Mac upgrade to Sequoia?
items.answer: No. Some users should stay on an older macOS if graphics, wireless, or performance caveats matter.

items.question: Is this a Sequoia installer guide?
items.answer: No. It is a compatibility planning page and links users back to official OCLP documentation.
